encounter.location: Triangle Pediatrics, 4201 Lake Boone Trail, Suite 200, Raleigh, NC 27607

conditions.name: recurrent viral-induced wheezing
conditions.note: Third wheezing episode in 8 weeks. Prior episodes managed with PRN albuterol; this episode more severe with incomplete response to home albuterol and overnight symptoms. Likely trajectory toward persistent asthma diagnosis; formal diagnosis deferred to upcoming 3-year well-child visit.

conditions.name: acute viral upper respiratory infection
conditions.note: Low-grade fever, rhinorrhea, pharyngeal erythema. Viral trigger for current wheezing exacerbation.

conditions.name: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis

medications.name: prednisolone 12 mg oral solution
medications.sig: prednisolone 12 mg (1 mg/kg/day) by mouth once daily for 3 days

medications.name: albuterol (ProAir HFA) 90 mcg metered dose inhaler
medications.sig: 2 puffs inhaled every 4-6 hours as needed for wheezing, via spacer with mask

document_references.description: Acute office visit note documenting evaluation and management of third wheezing episode in 8 weeks, including history of worsening response to home albuterol with overnight symptoms, exam findings of bilateral expiratory wheezing with subcostal retractions, partial response to in-office nebulization, assessment of recurrent viral-induced wheezing with discussion of likely trajectory toward persistent asthma diagnosis, and plan including prednisolone course with follow-up at 3-year well-child visit.

Written instructions on wheezing management at home and when to seek emergency care, including warning signs such as worsening respiratory distress, retractions, inability to feed, oxygen saturation below 92%, and poor response to albuterol.
